summary: The test suite is excellent overall. All core requirements are covered: glob file discovery (Test 1), match finding with line info (Test 2), preview mode without file modification (Test 3), backup creation (Test 4), no-backup path (Test 5), multi-file directory runs with summary (Test 6), preview mode in directory run (Test 7), context lines (Test 8), regex capture groups (Test 9), and no-match empty report (Test 10). Edge cases are reasonably well covered — no-match scenario, files without changes excluded from summary, backup overwrite behavior, and capture group substitutions. The design is clean: IDisposable-based temp directory fixture ensures test isolation, a reusable CreateFile helper keeps tests readable, tests are clearly labeled with TDD cycle comments, and each test has a focused single responsibility. Minor gaps preventing a perfect rigor score: no tests for invalid/nonexistent file paths, no concurrent-access or very large file edge cases, no test that backup is not created in preview mode when backup flag is set (only tested with backup=false in preview), and no test verifying the summary IsPreview flag is set correctly. But these are minor omissions in an otherwise thorough suite.
